title: Discovery and Development of first-in-class ACKR3/CXCR7 Superagonists for Platelet Degranulation Modulation
author: Antti Poso
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Molecular Dynamics trajectories of  ACKR3 bound to compound 27. 

 Desmond MD engine was used for the MD simulation with OPLS4 force field. The system was solvated in an orthorhombic box (minimum distance of 12 Å to the edges from the protein). The POPC (1-palmitoyl-2- oleylphosphatidylcholine) lipid bilayer was the membrane of choice for MD. The membrane was placed automatically on helices with further manual validation of transmembrane atoms. A temperature of 300 K was used for membrane patch pre-equilibration. The water was described with the TIP3P model. The final systems comprised ~44 k atoms. All simulations were run in the NpT ensemble (T = 310 K, Nosé-Hoover method; p = 1.01325 bar, Martyna-Tobias-Klein method) with default Desmond settings. Reversible reference system propagator algorithms (RESPA) integrator with 2 fs, 2 fs, and 6 fs timesteps were used for bonded/non-bonded interactions, near and far, respectively. The default value of 9 Å was used for the Coulombic cut-off. The system was relaxed using the default Desmond protocol before the production simulation. Five replicas of production simulations for the ACKR3– 27  were carried out with the same settings as mentioned above. Each system was simulated for 500 ns (5 × 500 ns = 2.5 μs). For each replica, a random seed was used. 

 Replica names are allocated as following: 

 110 . replica 1 

 110r1 - replica 2 

 110r2 - replica 3 

 110r3 - replica 4 

 110r4 - replica 5 

 Figure 5 Pymol session attached.
